description: Lando v4 Phase 1 MVP (walking skeleton): one developer can run `lando start` against one app with one service on Linux x64, end-to-end, via the Lando-managed runtime (private Podman socket). Stories follow the 7-PRD breakdown in `spec/mvp/prd-mvp-*.md`; each story has a corresponding US-XXX section in its PRD with full TDD acceptance criteria. Source of truth: `spec/ROADMAP.md` Phase 1.

userStories.title: Lock BootstrapLevel schema export from @lando/sdk
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want a single semver-stable BootstrapLevel literal exported from @lando/sdk/schema so I can declare the bootstrap depth my command needs without depending on @lando/core internals. (PRD-01 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/schema/bootstrap.test.ts` asserts BootstrapLevel is a Schema.Literal with exactly: none | minimal | plugins | commands | tooling | provider | global | scratch | app
Test asserts unknown literals fail decoding with a structured ParseError
BOOTSTRAP_RANK exported alongside, with a frozen ordering test that fails if any rank value changes

userStories.title: Ship AppRef, AppPlan, ServicePlan schemas in @lando/sdk
userStories.description: As @lando/core, I need full Effect Schemas for AppRef, AppPlan, and ServicePlan so the planner and providers consume a shared, validated shape instead of structurally-typed interfaces. (PRD-01 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/schema/app-plan.test.ts` decodes a fixture matching the minimal MVP shape (one app, one service, one mount, one endpoint)
Test asserts decoding rejects malformed plans with a structured ParseError
Schemas exported from `sdk/src/schema/` and re-exported from `@lando/sdk/schema`

userStories.title: Ship ProviderCapabilities schema (full shape) in @lando/sdk
userStories.description: As a provider author, I need a full ProviderCapabilities schema so my Live Layer declares its capabilities once and core/planner can dispatch on them. (PRD-01 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/schema/provider-capabilities.test.ts` asserts every field from spec/05-runtime-providers.md capability matrix (bindMountPerformance, sharedCrossAppNetwork, copyOnWriteAppRoot, etc.)
Test decodes a provider-lando fixture (`bindMountPerformance: native`) and a provider-docker fixture (`bindMountPerformance: slow`)
Required vs optional fields match the spec exactly; defaults come from decode

userStories.title: Ship landofile parse-input schemas (LandofileShape, GlobalConfig)
userStories.description: As @lando/core, I need a frozen schema for the parsed Landofile shape and GlobalConfig so LandofileService and ConfigService work against a stable contract. (PRD-01 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/schema/landofile.test.ts` decodes a minimal MVP Landofile (name, one service, Compose-subset keys: image, ports, environment, volumes, command, dependsOn)
Test asserts decoding rejects Compose keys outside the MVP allowlist with a LandofileValidationError naming the rejected key
GlobalConfig schema covers: userDataRoot, userConfRoot, defaultProviderId, telemetry.enabled (default false at MVP)

userStories.title: Ship the five MVP-mandated tagged errors in @lando/sdk
userStories.description: As a plugin author or library consumer, I need stable Effect TaggedError types for the failure modes Phase 1 publishes: LandoRuntimeBootstrapError, ProviderCapabilityError, LandofileParseError, PluginLoadError, NoProviderInstalledError. (PRD-01 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/errors/tagged-errors.test.ts` constructs each of the five errors and asserts both `_tag` discriminator and structured payload

Each error carries the payload fields needed for downstream rendering (e.g. LandofileParseError: { filePath, message, line?, column? }; ProviderCapabilityError: { providerId, capability, requiredValue, actualValue })
Test asserts Effect.failCause paths preserve `_tag` after Effect.runPromiseExit

userStories.title: Ship lifecycle event payload schemas (8-10 events for app:start/app:stop)
userStories.description: As an event subscriber (plugins, telemetry, future renderer), I need frozen payload shapes for the lifecycle events app:start/app:stop publish per spec/03-architecture.md §3.5. (PRD-01 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/events/lifecycle.test.ts` decodes payloads for: pre-app-start, post-app-start, pre-service-start, post-service-start, pre-app-stop, post-app-stop, pre-service-stop, post-service-stop, pre-build, post-build
Each payload schema includes: appRef, serviceName? (services-scoped only), providerId, timestamp
Each event has a literal `eventName` field that pins the discriminator

userStories.title: Ship Effect service tags with frozen interface shapes in @lando/sdk
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I need Effect Service tags (Context.Tag class-extending pattern) for every service Phase 1 ships, with frozen method shapes. (PRD-01 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/services/tags.test.ts` asserts each tag is exported, is a Context.Tag, has documented method names with Effect-typed return signatures: Logger, EventService, RuntimeProvider, RuntimeProviderRegistry, ConfigService, LandofileService, PluginRegistry, CacheService, FileSystem, ProcessRunner, ShellRunner
Each method's failure channel is typed with one or more SDK tagged errors (no unknown, no bare Error)

userStories.title: Provider contract test stub in @lando/sdk/test (with TestRuntimeProvider)
userStories.description: As a provider author, I need a published contract test suite I can run my Live Layer through to prove conformance to the RuntimeProvider interface. A trivial in-memory TestRuntimeProvider proves the suite runs. (PRD-01 US-008)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/contract/provider.test.ts` documents Phase 1 contract assertions (capability matrix exists, bringUp/bringDown Effect-typed, exec returns Stream of stdio chunks, logs returns Stream<LogChunk>, inspect returns structured snapshot)
Suite exported from `@lando/sdk/test` as `runProviderContract(provider): Effect<void, ContractFailure>`
Trivial in-memory TestRuntimeProvider in `sdk/src/test/` passes the suite

userStories.title: Verify @lando/sdk package exports surface
userStories.description: As an embedding host, I need every PRD-mandated entry point to resolve cleanly so I can pin against a stable surface. (PRD-01 US-009)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `sdk/test/library/exports.test.ts` does `await import('@lando/sdk')`, '/schema', '/errors', '/events', '/services', '/test' and asserts each resolves with the expected canonical sample symbol

userStories.title: makeLandoRuntime factory composes a Layer at the requested bootstrap level
userStories.description: As @lando/core's OCLIF init hook, I need makeLandoRuntime({ bootstrap: <level> }) to return one composed Layer that satisfies every required service tag for that level — and only that level. (PRD-02 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/runtime/make-runtime.test.ts` calls makeLandoRuntime({ bootstrap: 'minimal' }) and asserts the returned Layer satisfies Logger | ConfigService | FileSystem but not RuntimeProvider
Same test for bootstrap: 'provider' satisfies RuntimeProvider | RuntimeProviderRegistry
Same test for bootstrap: 'app' satisfies LandofileService | AppPlanner | RuntimeProvider
Failure cases (invalid bootstrap value, malformed options) fail with LandoRuntimeBootstrapError carrying a structured payload

userStories.title: OCLIF init hook resolves command's bootstrap level and provides matching Layer
userStories.description: As an OCLIF command, I declare `static bootstrap: BootstrapLevel = 'provider'` on my class; the init hook reads that level on dispatch and provides a runtime that satisfies it. (PRD-02 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/init-hook.test.ts` registers a fixture command with `static bootstrap = 'provider'`, dispatches via OCLIF, and asserts hook ran before run() and the command can `yield* RuntimeProvider`
Second fixture with `static bootstrap = 'minimal'` cannot `yield* RuntimeProvider` without missing-context error
Init hook lives at `core/src/cli/oclif/hooks/init.ts`, registered in core/package.json oclif.hooks
Hook fails with LandoRuntimeBootstrapError if a command class is missing `static bootstrap` (no silent default)

userStories.title: Pre-OCLIF fast path for --version, -v, version
userStories.description: As a CI script that runs `lando --version` thousands of times, that path must skip OCLIF + Effect entirely and return in <50ms. (PRD-02 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/fast-path.test.ts` shells out to `bun run core/src/cli/index.ts --version`, asserts exit 0 and stdout matches version from core/package.json
Test asserts no Effect runtime imports were touched (debug hook throws if makeLandoRuntime is called)
Wall-clock budget ≤50ms documented (not enforced at MVP)

userStories.title: Pre-OCLIF fast path for shellenv at bootstrap level none
userStories.description: As a user setting up shell integration, `lando shellenv` must run at bootstrap level `none` so it works before any lando state exists on the machine. (PRD-02 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/fast-path.test.ts` runs `lando shellenv` against compiled binary and asserts stdout contains canonical shellenv lines (export of LANDO_INSTALL_DIR, prepend `${LANDO_INSTALL_DIR}/bin` to PATH)
Output identical regardless of whether ~/.lando/ exists
Fast-path branch handles `shellenv` without invoking Effect runtime

userStories.title: BUNDLED_PLUGINS array consumed by PluginRegistry's bundled-only loader
userStories.description: As PluginRegistry.Live, I read the BUNDLED_PLUGINS array from `core/src/plugins/bundled.ts` to decide which plugins are loaded at MVP — no FS scanning, no dynamic imports. (PRD-02 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/plugins/bundled.test.ts` asserts BUNDLED_PLUGINS exported as ReadonlyArray<{ name, layer, manifest }> containing all four MVP plugins (@lando/provider-lando, @lando/provider-docker, @lando/service-lando, @lando/logger-pretty)
Empty layer/manifest stubs are acceptable here; PRDs 04 and 05 fill them
Test asserts bundled.ts is regenerated by `scripts/build-bundled-plugins.ts` (idempotent on fresh checkout)

userStories.title: scripts/codegen.ts orchestrates all codegen steps
userStories.description: As a developer running `bun run codegen`, every codegen output (bundled.ts, OCLIF manifest, schema artifacts, recipe asset embedding, CI workflow) is regenerated in one command. (PRD-02 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/scripts/codegen.test.ts` runs `bun run codegen` and asserts all expected output files exist and are byte-identical to a fresh run (idempotency)
scripts/codegen.ts invokes scripts/build-bundled-plugins.ts and scripts/build-ci-workflow.ts (CI workflow gen lives in PRD-07 US-005)
`bun run codegen` is the single command developers run

userStories.title: Compiled binary builds for Linux x64 via bun build --compile
userStories.description: As release engineering, `bun run build` produces a single Linux x64 binary at `dist/lando` that runs on the dev's machine. (PRD-02 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/build/build-binary.test.ts` runs `bun run build`, asserts `dist/lando` exists, is executable, and `./dist/lando --version` returns the expected version
package.json `build` script invokes `bun build --compile` with the right entrypoint

userStories.title: Test harness for Effect services (test-runner helper)
userStories.description: As a service-test author, I want a thin helper that builds a Layer of in-memory test doubles for the SDK service tags so writing service tests is one import, not boilerplate. (PRD-02 US-008)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/runtime/test-harness.test.ts` imports `@lando/core/testing` makeTestRuntime() and asserts it provides Logger, FileSystem, ProcessRunner, ConfigService as in-memory doubles
Doubles record calls so tests can assert on them

userStories.title: bun test configuration covers unit + Effect-service + CLI scenario tests
userStories.description: As a developer running `bun test`, every test layer (unit, Effect service, CLI scenario, integration) discovers and runs without extra config. (PRD-02 US-009)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test (or build assertion) verifies `bun test` discovers tests under `core/test/`, `sdk/test/`, `plugins/*/test/`
Integration tests gated behind env var (e.g. `LANDO_TEST_PODMAN_SOCKET`) are skipped without that var
`bun test` exit code is 0 on a fresh checkout

userStories.title: tsc -b build-info file dist/.tsbuildinfo does not leak into runtime
userStories.description: As a packager, I need `tsc -b` artifacts (.tsbuildinfo, dist/) to stay out of the published package and the compiled binary. (PRD-02 US-010)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/build/no-leak.test.ts` greps the compiled `dist/lando` binary for `.tsbuildinfo` strings and asserts none are present
`.gitignore` and `.npmignore` (if any) exclude tsc build artifacts
`bun run clean` removes dist/ and .tsbuildinfo files

userStories.title: biome check . is enforced (lint gate)
userStories.description: As a developer, `bun run lint` runs biome with the repo's biome.json and exits non-zero on any violation. (PRD-02 US-011)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test or CI assertion: `bun run lint` exits 0 on a clean checkout
Introducing a deliberate lint violation makes `bun run lint` exit non-zero
biome.json governs the rules; no per-file overrides without justification

userStories.title: ProcessRunner Live implementation (Bun.spawn)
userStories.description: As any service that needs to run a child process, I depend on the ProcessRunner tag and get a Bun.spawn-backed Live impl that returns Effect-typed exit/stdio. (PRD-03 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/process-runner.test.ts` runs a trivial command (e.g. `echo hi`) via ProcessRunner and asserts exit code 0 and captured stdout 'hi'
Failure (non-zero exit) yields a tagged error with structured stderr/exit payload
Stream-mode call returns a Stream<{ kind: 'stdout' | 'stderr', chunk: Uint8Array }>

userStories.title: ShellRunner Live implementation (Bun.$)
userStories.description: As the host ToolingEngine and CLI scenario tests, I need a ShellRunner Live impl backed by Bun.$ that returns Effect-typed results. (PRD-03 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/shell-runner.test.ts` runs `echo hi` via ShellRunner and asserts captured stdout
Failure exits propagate as tagged errors with payload
Cwd, env, and shell options surfaced in the API

userStories.title: FileSystem Live implementation (Bun.file)
userStories.description: As any service that reads/writes files, I depend on FileSystem and get a Bun.file-backed Live impl. (PRD-03 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/file-system.test.ts` writes a temp file via FileSystem.write, reads it back via FileSystem.read, asserts equal content
Read of nonexistent path yields tagged error with path payload

userStories.title: ConfigService Live implementation (global config + env overlay)
userStories.description: As consumers of global config, I get ConfigService.Live that loads from `<userConfRoot>/lando.yml` with environment-variable overlay. (PRD-03 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/config-service.test.ts` writes a temp lando.yml, asserts ConfigService loads expected values
Env var overlay (e.g. LANDO_DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID) takes precedence over yml
Decoded against GlobalConfig schema; rejects bad yml with tagged error

userStories.title: LandofileService Live implementation (YAML + Compose subset)
userStories.description: As AppPlanner, I depend on LandofileService.Live to parse `.lando.yml` against the MVP Compose-subset allowlist. No includes, no landofile.ts, no expressions at MVP. (PRD-03 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/landofile-service.test.ts` parses a minimal Node+Postgres .lando.yml and asserts the structured shape matches the Landofile schema
Compose keys outside the MVP allowlist are rejected with LandofileParseError naming the rejected key
YAML errors propagate as LandofileParseError with line/column

userStories.title: EventService Live implementation (basic pub/sub)
userStories.description: As app:start/app:stop, I depend on EventService.Live to publish lifecycle events; subscribers receive them in order. No priority bands at MVP. (PRD-03 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/event-service.test.ts` subscribes a listener, publishes pre-app-start and post-app-start, asserts both received in order with correct payloads
Multiple subscribers all receive the event
Subscriber error does not abort other subscribers

userStories.title: Logger Live implementation (Effect Logger.pretty wrapper)
userStories.description: As any service, I depend on Logger.Live and get Effect's built-in Logger.pretty under the hood with the SDK's Logger interface on top. (PRD-03 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/logger.test.ts` logs at info, warn, error levels and asserts the rendered output matches expected pretty format
Logger.Live is provided by the minimal bootstrap level

userStories.title: CacheService Live implementation (in-memory)
userStories.description: As any caller that needs caching, I depend on CacheService.Live which is in-memory only at MVP (persistent caches are Alpha). (PRD-03 US-008)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/cache-service.test.ts` sets a key, reads it back, asserts roundtrip
TTL semantics (if any at MVP) work as documented

userStories.title: PluginRegistry Live implementation (bundled-only)
userStories.description: As @lando/core, PluginRegistry.Live reads BUNDLED_PLUGINS at startup — no FS discovery, no install, no dynamic imports at MVP. (PRD-03 US-009)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/plugin-registry.test.ts` asserts PluginRegistry.list() returns all four bundled plugins
Unknown plugin id yields tagged PluginLoadError

userStories.title: RuntimeProvider + RuntimeProviderRegistry Live implementations
userStories.description: As app:start, I depend on RuntimeProvider (the picked one) and RuntimeProviderRegistry (the picker). Live impls dispatch by id; missing provider yields NoProviderInstalledError. (PRD-03 US-010)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/runtime-provider-registry.test.ts` registers a TestRuntimeProvider, picks it by id, asserts dispatch
Default provider id resolves from ConfigService.defaultProviderId

userStories.title: AppPlanner Live implementation (minimal AppPlan)
userStories.description: As app:start, I depend on AppPlanner.Live that takes a parsed Landofile and emits a minimal AppPlan with services, one mount (app root bind), and endpoints. (PRD-03 US-011)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/app-planner.test.ts` plans a Node+Postgres Landofile and asserts the resulting AppPlan has 2 services with expected images, 1 app-root bind mount, and per-service endpoints
Plan is decoded against AppPlan schema

userStories.title: BuildOrchestrator Live implementation (sequential)
userStories.description: As app:start, I depend on BuildOrchestrator.Live which sequentially runs pre-build → build → post-build for each service. No group weighting at MVP. (PRD-03 US-012)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/services/build-orchestrator.test.ts` orchestrates a 2-service plan and asserts pre-build / post-build events are published per service in order
Failure of one service short-circuits the orchestration with the failing service's error

userStories.title: Create @lando/provider-lando workspace package skeleton
userStories.description: As the bundled providers list, I need the @lando/provider-lando workspace package to exist with a Layer export and a manifest, ready to be filled by subsequent stories. (PRD-04 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/provider-lando/test/skeleton.test.ts` imports the package, asserts a Layer is exported, asserts the manifest declares pluginId @lando/provider-lando

userStories.title: provider-lando capability matrix introspection
userStories.description: As @lando/core/planner, I depend on provider-lando declaring its ProviderCapabilities at registration. On Linux: bindMountPerformance: native, sharedCrossAppNetwork: false (MVP). (PRD-04 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/provider-lando/test/capabilities.test.ts` registers the Live Layer and asserts ProviderCapabilities matches the spec for Linux MVP
Capabilities are populated via Podman API introspection (not from podman binary on PATH)

userStories.title: provider-lando Compose file emission to per-app temp dir
userStories.description: As provider-lando.bringUp, I emit a Compose file for the app to a per-app temp dir (internal only, never user-exposed). (PRD-04 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/provider-lando/test/compose-emit.test.ts` plans a 2-service AppPlan and asserts the emitted Compose YAML matches expected shape (services, networks, volumes)
File location is under a per-app temp dir, not the app root
Compose file references images and ports from the AppPlan

userStories.title: provider-lando bringUp brings services to running state
userStories.description: As app:start, I call provider-lando.bringUp(plan) and it brings every service to running state via the private Podman API socket. (PRD-04 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `plugins/provider-lando/test/bring-up.integration.test.ts` (gated on LANDO_TEST_PODMAN_SOCKET on Linux x64) brings up a Node+Postgres plan and asserts both containers are running via API inspect
Bun.spawn-driven RPC against the socket — not `podman` binary on PATH
bringUp is idempotent: calling twice on the same plan is a no-op the second time
Test functionality against live Podman socket

userStories.title: provider-lando bringDown stops + removes containers
userStories.description: As app:stop, I call provider-lando.bringDown(plan) and every container the plan provisioned is stopped and removed (volumes preserved). (PRD-04 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `plugins/provider-lando/test/bring-down.integration.test.ts` brings up a plan, brings it down, asserts no containers remain via API inspect
Volumes declared `scope: app` are preserved across bringDown/bringUp cycles
Test functionality against live Podman socket

userStories.title: provider-lando exec returns Stream of stdio chunks
userStories.description: As future tooling, I can call provider-lando.exec(plan, service, cmd) and get back a Stream<{kind, chunk}> for stdout/stderr plus an Effect-typed exit code. (PRD-04 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `plugins/provider-lando/test/exec.integration.test.ts` brings up a plan, execs `echo hi` in the node service, asserts captured stdout 'hi' and exit 0
Stream-based API; no buffering whole output before yield
Failure (nonzero exit) propagates as a tagged error with exit code in payload
Test functionality against live Podman socket

userStories.title: provider-lando logs returns follow Stream
userStories.description: As `lando logs` (future), I depend on provider-lando.logs(plan, service, { follow }) which yields a Stream<LogChunk>. (PRD-04 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `plugins/provider-lando/test/logs.integration.test.ts` brings up a service that prints to stdout, calls logs({ follow: false }), asserts captured chunks contain expected lines
follow: true returns an open stream that surfaces new chunks until canceled
Test functionality against live Podman socket

userStories.title: provider-lando inspect returns structured snapshot
userStories.description: As `lando info`, I depend on provider-lando.inspect(plan) returning a structured snapshot (services + container ids + states + endpoints). (PRD-04 US-008)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `plugins/provider-lando/test/inspect.integration.test.ts` brings up a plan, calls inspect, asserts the returned snapshot matches expected schema (services, ids, states, endpoints)
inspect is read-only — does not mutate provider state
Test functionality against live Podman socket

userStories.title: provider-lando passes the SDK provider contract suite
userStories.description: As a provider author, I run runProviderContract(provider-lando) from @lando/sdk/test and it passes every Phase 1 contract assertion. (PRD-04 US-009)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/provider-lando/test/contract.integration.test.ts` runs runProviderContract against the Live Layer and asserts pass
Test runs in CI on Linux x64 against a private Podman socket
Test functionality against live Podman socket

userStories.title: @lando/provider-docker Live Layer (Linux Docker Engine, stretch)
userStories.description: As a contributor with Docker Engine running, I get a working escape-hatch RuntimeProvider so the contract suite is cross-validated. (PRD-04 US-010)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/provider-docker/test/contract.integration.test.ts` (gated on Docker socket presence) runs runProviderContract against the docker Live Layer and asserts pass
Capability matrix declares bindMountPerformance: native (Linux Engine) and sharedCrossAppNetwork: false (MVP)
Compose emission + `docker exec` + `docker logs --follow` via Bun.spawn

userStories.title: lando setup (provider-lando) — manual-install acceptance
userStories.description: As a dev on Linux, `lando setup` for provider-lando at MVP accepts a system-installed Podman (manual install required); it surfaces a clear remediation message if Podman is missing. (PRD-04 US-011)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/provider-lando/test/setup.integration.test.ts` runs setup with no podman socket, asserts it exits with NoProviderInstalledError carrying remediation message
With socket present, setup succeeds and reports detected Podman version
No automatic download of pinned Podman binary at MVP (deferred to Alpha; documented as such)

userStories.title: Provider-lando does not import @oclif/core (boundary test)
userStories.description: As an embedding library consumer, plugins must not pull OCLIF into the default entry point. (PRD-04 US-012)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/provider-lando/test/boundary.test.ts` asserts no source under plugins/provider-lando/src imports from @oclif/core (transitive or direct)

userStories.title: @lando/service-lando package skeleton
userStories.description: As the bundled-services list, I need the @lando/service-lando workspace package to exist with a Layer export and a manifest. (PRD-05 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/service-lando/test/skeleton.test.ts` imports the package, asserts a Layer is exported, manifest declares pluginId @lando/service-lando

userStories.title: node:lts ServiceType implementation
userStories.description: As app:start, I get a working ServiceType `node:lts` that produces a Node service with default image, default port, and Compose-subset overrides. (PRD-05 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/service-lando/test/node-service-type.test.ts` plans a Landofile with `services: { web: { type: 'node:lts' } }`, asserts ServicePlan output (image: node:lts, port mapping, default command)
User overrides (image, environment, command) propagate from Landofile to the ServicePlan

userStories.title: postgres ServiceType implementation
userStories.description: As app:start, I get a working ServiceType `postgres` that produces a Postgres service with default image, default port, and Compose-subset overrides. (PRD-05 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/service-lando/test/postgres-service-type.test.ts` plans a Landofile with `services: { db: { type: 'postgres' } }`, asserts ServicePlan output (image: postgres, port mapping, default env vars POSTGRES_USER/POSTGRES_PASSWORD/POSTGRES_DB)
A scope:app named volume is declared for /var/lib/postgresql/data so data persists across bringDown/bringUp

userStories.title: service-lando Layer wires both ServiceTypes into core's registry
userStories.description: As @lando/core, when service-lando's Layer is provided, both `node:lts` and `postgres` ServiceTypes appear in the planner's ServiceType registry. (PRD-05 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/service-lando/test/registry.test.ts` provides the service-lando Layer and asserts both ServiceTypes are registered and resolvable by id
Unknown service type in a Landofile yields a structured ParseError naming the unknown type

userStories.title: service-lando does not import @oclif/core (boundary test)
userStories.description: As an embedding library consumer, service plugins must not pull OCLIF into the default entry. (PRD-05 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/service-lando/test/boundary.test.ts` asserts no source under plugins/service-lando/src imports from @oclif/core

userStories.title: @lando/logger-pretty package skeleton (empty body)
userStories.description: As the bundled plugin set, @lando/logger-pretty exists with a manifest and an empty body — Effect's default Logger.pretty is good enough at MVP. The package exists so the bundled.ts codegen path is exercised. (PRD-05 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `plugins/logger-pretty/test/skeleton.test.ts` imports the package, asserts a Layer is exported (empty bind on Logger is acceptable), manifest declares pluginId @lando/logger-pretty

userStories.title: BUNDLED_PLUGINS codegen handles all four MVP plugins
userStories.description: As `bun run codegen`, scripts/build-bundled-plugins.ts emits a bundled.ts referencing all four MVP plugins by their actual Layer/manifest exports (not stubs). (PRD-05 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/plugins/bundled-codegen.test.ts` runs scripts/build-bundled-plugins.ts and asserts bundled.ts re-exports real Layer + manifest from each of the four MVP packages
Compiled binary contains all four plugins (no FS dynamic import)

userStories.title: lando init --full --name=<name> scaffolds a Node+Postgres app
userStories.description: As a developer, `lando init --full --name=mvp` creates a directory containing a working Node+Postgres .lando.yml from the single hardcoded MVP recipe. No prompts beyond --name. (PRD-06 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/init.scenario.test.ts` runs `lando init --full --name=mvp`, asserts a `mvp/` directory exists with .lando.yml + minimal Node app file (e.g. server.js)
Re-running init in same dir is a no-op (or yields a clear error)
Run `./dist/lando init --full --name=mvp-test` against compiled binary and verify output

userStories.title: lando start brings up the scaffolded app via provider-lando
userStories.description: As a developer in a scaffolded app dir, `lando start` plans the app, calls provider-lando.bringUp, and reports running services. (PRD-06 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `core/test/cli/start.integration.test.ts` (gated on LANDO_TEST_PODMAN_SOCKET) scaffolds a fresh app via init, runs start, asserts both services are running via inspect
Lifecycle events pre-app-start, post-app-start, pre-service-start (×2), post-service-start (×2) are published
Plain text output reports the started services (no concurrent task tree at MVP)
Run `./dist/lando start` end-to-end against live Podman socket

userStories.title: lando stop brings down the running app
userStories.description: As a developer in a running app, `lando stop` calls provider-lando.bringDown and reports clean stop. (PRD-06 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `core/test/cli/stop.integration.test.ts` starts an app, runs stop, asserts no containers remain via inspect
Lifecycle events pre-app-stop, post-app-stop, pre-service-stop (×2), post-service-stop (×2) are published
Run `./dist/lando stop` end-to-end against live Podman socket

userStories.title: lando info prints services + endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, `lando info` calls provider-lando.inspect and prints the structured snapshot in plain text (services with state and endpoints). (PRD-06 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing integration test `core/test/cli/info.integration.test.ts` starts an app, runs info, asserts stdout contains both service names and their endpoint URLs
info on a stopped app reports services as stopped (no error)
Run `./dist/lando info` end-to-end against live Podman socket

userStories.title: lando version works at bootstrap level none
userStories.description: As a CI script, `lando version` runs at bootstrap level `none` (Effect runtime never instantiated) and prints version from package.json. (PRD-06 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/version.test.ts` runs the compiled binary `lando version`, asserts exit 0 and stdout matches version

userStories.title: lando shellenv works at bootstrap level none
userStories.description: As a user setting up shell integration, `lando shellenv` runs at bootstrap level `none` and prints export lines for LANDO_INSTALL_DIR + PATH prepend. (PRD-06 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/shellenv.test.ts` runs `lando shellenv`, asserts stdout contains canonical shellenv lines
Output identical regardless of whether ~/.lando/ exists (same as PRD-02 US-004)

userStories.title: Every non-MVP OCLIF command exits with structured NotImplementedError
userStories.description: As a user who runs a not-yet-shipped command, I get a clear NotImplementedError with the command id and a 'this is alpha/beta/...' remediation message — not a stack trace. (PRD-06 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/not-implemented.test.ts` runs a representative non-MVP command (e.g. `lando exec`) and asserts exit non-zero plus stderr contains NotImplementedError with the command id
Every non-MVP OCLIF command class throws NotImplementedError consistently

userStories.title: app:start / app:stop / app:info command aliases
userStories.description: As an embedding script that wants explicit command ids, `app:start`, `app:stop`, `app:info` are valid aliases of `start`, `stop`, `info`. (PRD-06 US-008)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/cli/aliases.test.ts` runs `app:start`, `app:stop`, `app:info` against a scaffolded app and asserts they behave identically to their unprefixed counterparts

userStories.title: End-to-end MVP exit-criteria smoke test
userStories.description: As release engineering, I want a single-script reproduction of the roadmap's MVP exit criteria runnable in a temp dir on a Linux x64 box with a private Podman socket. (PRD-06 US-009)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing scenario test `core/test/scenario/mvp-exit-criteria.scenario.test.ts` (gated on LANDO_TEST_PODMAN_SOCKET, linux x64) runs in a temp dir: bun run codegen → bun run build → ./dist/lando init --full → ./dist/lando start → ./dist/lando info (asserts both services running) → ./dist/lando stop → cleanup
Test passes once every prior story is green
Run the full smoke flow end-to-end against live Podman socket

userStories.title: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and gates static-checks pipeline
userStories.description: As a maintainer, every PR runs a static-checks job that runs typecheck + lint + non-integration `bun test` on Linux x64. (PRD-07 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/ci/static-checks.test.ts` parses .github/workflows/ci.yml and asserts a `static-checks` job exists with steps: bun install, bun run typecheck, bun run lint, bun test (filtering out *.integration.test.ts)
Job runs on ubuntu-22.04 (pinned, not latest, per cross-cutting risks)

userStories.title: CI runs binary build and uploads it as workflow artifact
userStories.description: As a maintainer, every PR has a `build-linux-x64` job that runs `bun build --compile`, runs `--version`/`--help` smoke checks, and uploads `dist/lando` as the `lando-linux-x64` workflow artifact (7-day retention). No GitHub Releases at MVP. (PRD-07 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/ci/build-linux-x64.test.ts` parses ci.yml and asserts a `build-linux-x64` job exists with build + smoke + upload-artifact steps
Job depends on static-checks (does not start until static gate passes)

userStories.title: CI runs full provider integration suite against a Podman socket
userStories.description: As a maintainer, every PR has a `provider-integration-linux-x64` job that starts a private Podman socket in the runner and runs every *.integration.test.ts plus the MVP exit-criteria scenario. (PRD-07 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/ci/provider-integration.test.ts` parses ci.yml and asserts a `provider-integration-linux-x64` job exists with steps: install podman, start socket, set LANDO_TEST_PODMAN_SOCKET env var, run integration test glob, run mvp-exit-criteria scenario

userStories.title: CI workflow is the only permitted source of truth for CI config
userStories.description: As a maintainer, hand-editing .github/workflows/ci.yml is forbidden — the file carries a generated-header comment and any drift is detected by a check. (PRD-07 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/ci/no-hand-edits.test.ts` asserts ci.yml carries a `# AUTO-GENERATED` header comment
Test runs scripts/build-ci-workflow.ts in dry-run and asserts the file matches the generated output (otherwise fails with diff)

userStories.title: scripts/codegen.ts regenerates the CI workflow
userStories.description: As a developer running `bun run codegen`, the CI workflow regen is one of the steps invoked. (PRD-07 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/ci/codegen-invokes-ci.test.ts` runs `bun run codegen`, asserts ci.yml is rewritten and matches what scripts/build-ci-workflow.ts produces standalone

userStories.title: CI failure modes are debuggable (logs + artifacts on failure)
userStories.description: As a maintainer debugging a red CI run, I get useful logs: typecheck/lint output, full bun test output, podman daemon log on integration failure, and the dist/lando binary uploaded even on failure. (PRD-07 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/ci/debuggability.test.ts` parses ci.yml and asserts: actions/upload-artifact uses `if: always()` for diagnostic uploads, podman-related step captures journalctl on failure, bun test runs without `--silent`

userStories.title: CI is required for merge to main (branch protection assertion)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, all three CI jobs (static-checks, build-linux-x64, provider-integration-linux-x64) are required status checks for merge to main. (PRD-07 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test `core/test/ci/branch-protection.test.ts` reads the documented required-checks list (in repo doc or via gh API mock) and asserts all three job names are required
Document the branch-protection requirement in README or CONTRIBUTING.md so it's discoverable
